title: AP Price Adjustment — DI Pipes Monthly Pig-Iron Formula
text_english: For DI PIPES, Price Adjustment shall be allowed on a MONTHLY BASIS using the formula: P2 = P1 + (CPI2 − CPI1) × M × {{pig_iron_factor}} / 1000; where: P2 = Revised pipe price in Rupees per Meter; P1 = Pipe Price in Rupees per Meter as per SSR / Agreement; CPI2 = Price of Pig Iron at the time of delivery; CPI1 = Price of Pig Iron considered in the estimate; M = Weight of pipe in Kgs per Meter as per IS:8329-1994; {{pig_iron_factor}} = {{pig_iron_factor_default}} (default 0.96) — i.e., 0.96 MT of Pig Iron is required for manufacture of 1 MT of DI Pipes.

title: AP Price Adjustment — Labour Component via CPI-IW
text_english: For LABOUR component of Price Adjustment, the adjustment shall be allowed on a MONTHLY BASIS using the CONSUMER PRICE INDEX FOR INDUSTRIAL WORKERS (CPI-IW) published by the Labour Department of the Government of India. The formula uses: Vl = Variation in labour cost (amount to be paid/deducted); R = Value of Work done during the specified month (Gross Bill Amount LESS CPOH LESS tender premium LESS reimbursable items such as seignorage and GST); Pp = Component of Labour expressed as a percentage of the total value of work excluding CPOH and Tender Premium; Lo = CPI-IW as on the date of sanction of Estimate; Li = CPI-IW as on the date of recording of measurements.
